headline: Anderson's arm overwhelms Georgia defense in Colorado's statement road victory
body: Colorado walked out of Georgia's house with a 42-28 victory that felt more decisive than the final margin suggests. Roger Anderson carved up the Georgia secondary with surgical precision, completing 24 of 38 passes for 350 yards and three touchdowns while committing just one fumble across the entire game. The Colorado quarterback's composure in the pocket gave his receivers time to work downfield, and when Georgia's defense tried to tighten up, Anderson found the intermediate routes that kept drives alive.

Georgia's Giles put up respectable numbers on the stat sheet—254 yards and four touchdown passes—but Colorado's pass rush, anchored by Carter's sack, created enough disruption to prevent any sustained rhythm. The Georgia offense moved the ball adequately but squandered opportunities in the red zone where efficiency mattered most. Colorado's defense bent but didn't break, forcing Georgia into low-percentage situations late while Anderson and his offense controlled the tempo on their end.

The turning point came when Colorado's receivers, particularly Mecum's 89-yard contribution, stretched the field vertically in the second half. Georgia's secondary appeared uncomfortable with the spacing and timing of Anderson's routes, and Colorado's run game, though not dominant, provided just enough balance to keep Georgia from loading the box. This was a complete performance from the visitors, one that suggests Colorado's coaching room has installed something that travels well.
turningPoint: Colorado's second-half vertical passing attack, highlighted by Mecum's play, exposed a Georgia secondary that couldn't maintain coverage consistency, allowing Anderson to find rhythm and control the game's tempo.

unsungHero.name: Sweeney
unsungHero.why: His 13 carries and rushing touchdown provided crucial balance that kept Georgia from blitzing freely against Anderson, yet the QB's aerial dominance overshadowed a workmanlike ground effort that was essential to controlling the game.
